sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section III, List of Answers on Terminology 9:1

items.sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section III, List of Answers on Terminology 9:1
items.html: When direct light, meaning light of Hochma, is not clothed in the vessel, then the vessel is called posterior, or posteriors. It has been written in item 4 that the light of Hochma is called the light of the face. Thus, when the light of Hochma is absent from the vessel, it is called posterior. (Part 3, Chapter 15, item 6)
